name: Los Llanos - Calderón Trail
name_original: Mirador de la Peñanegra – circular desde Piornal por la Ruta a la Peñanegra (PR-CC 13)
description: Hike the difficult 13.6-mile Los Llanos - Calderón Trail through a volcanic landscape, gaining 1871 feet to panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Piornal offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, forested valleys, and traditional agricultural areas within the Jerte Valley region. The terrain often features varied elevations, providing both gentle paths and more challenging ascents. Trails frequently pass through oak and chestnut forests, alongside streams, and offer views of the surrounding Extremaduran countryside. Best hiking trails around Piornal The most popular hiking route is Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32), a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to a notable waterfall, offering scenic views and a refreshing stop. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monasterio de San Jerónimo de Yuste – circular from Garganta la Olla by the Antiguo Camino a Yuste (PR-CC 80), a moderate 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This trail combines historical interest with varied forest and valley scenery, following an ancient route. Local hikers also love the Mojón Blanco Trail, a 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through varied forest and open hillside, often completed in about 4 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Piornal is defined by forested valleys, traditional paths, and panoramic views of the Jerte Valley. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Piornal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Piornal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Piornal?
A: Piornal offers a wide variety of hiking options, with over 60 trails to explore. These include 24 easy routes, 34 moderate, and 6 more challenging paths, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Piornal?
A: Yes, Piornal has 24 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990729084/los-llanos-calderon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Llanos - Calderón Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.4 miles (5.5 km) long and takes about 1 hour 48 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on Piornal's hiking routes?
A: Hiking around Piornal features diverse landscapes, from rolling hills and forested valleys to traditional agricultural areas within the Jerte Valley. Trails often pass through oak and chestnut forests, alongside streams, and provide panoramic views of the Extremaduran countryside. You'll encounter varied elevations, offering both gentle paths and more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Piornal?
A: Yes, Piornal offers several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990727089/cascada-del-caozo-circular-from-valdastillas-pr-cc-32" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that leads to a beautiful waterfall.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or viewpoints to see while hiking near Piornal?
A: The area around Piornal is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; on the Jerte River also offer a refreshing stop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Piornal?
A: The trails in Piornal are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, the well-maintained paths through forests and valleys, and the opportunities to discover natural attractions like waterfalls and viewpoints.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, some trails combine natural beauty with historical interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990720304/monasterio-de-san-jeronimo-de-yuste-circular-from-garganta-la-olla-by-the-antiguo-camino-a-yuste-pr-cc-80" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de San Jerónimo de Yuste – circular from Garganta la Olla&lt;/a&gt; follows an ancient route and offers a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any trails that offer opportunities for wild swimming or natural pools?
A: Yes, the Jerte Valley is known for its natural swimming spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; are a popular destination for a refreshing dip, often accessible via hiking routes in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Piornal?
A: The Jerte Valley, where Piornal is located, is beautiful year-round. Spring is particularly stunning with the cherry blossoms, while autumn offers vibrant fall foliage. Summer can be warm, but trails through forested areas provide shade, and natural pools offer relief. Winter hiking is also possible, with cooler temperatures and a different kind of beauty.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options if I prefer a quieter experience?
A: While popular routes like the Cascada del Caozo can attract visitors, Piornal has over 60 trails, many of which offer a more tranquil experience. Exploring some of the less-known moderate or difficult trails, or hiking during off-peak hours, can lead to a quieter adventure through the region's forests and hillsides.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints for photography?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Piornal offer excellent viewpoints overlooking the Jerte Valley and the surrounding Extremaduran countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is specifically noted for its scenic vistas, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990739666/mojon-blanco-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojón Blanco Trail&lt;/a&gt; traverse open hillsides with expansive views.
tips: The top of Peñanegra is located at 1,393 meters above sea level and offers wonderful views of the entire Navaconcejo region to the north and behind it the Piornal region, where you can appreciate the beautiful landscape of Extremadura. The ascent can be difficult because it is a long journey, go prepared with plenty of water and sun protection if you do it on hot or summer days.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail?
faqs.answer: The Los Llanos - Calderón Trail is generally considered easy to moderately difficult. While much of the path is graveled, you will encounter rocky and uneven sections, especially near lava tube caves. The ascent to the top of El Calderón involves wooden steps, and the cinder cone itself can be covered in sharp scoria. It's suitable for most ages, but some areas have steep drop-offs.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse volcanic landscape. You'll traverse ancient lava flows, walk on graveled paths, and navigate rocky sections. The path also includes wooden steps for ascending the cinder cone, where the ground can be covered in sharp scoria. Expect a mix of forest paths and open volcanic expanses.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is a geologic wonderland, showcasing ancient lava flows, winding trenches formed by lava rivers, and lava tubes. You'll pass through piñon-juniper forests and stands of ponderosa pines. A significant highlight is the opportunity to ascend a 300-foot tall extinct cinder cone volcano, offering panoramic views of the volcanic landscape. Don't miss the Peñanegra Viewpoint for stunning vistas.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail?
faqs.answer: The diverse ecosystems along the trail support a variety of wildlife. You might spot piñon jays, rabbits, snakes, and lizards. Larger mammals such as deer, elk, black bears, and even mountain lions are also present. During warmer months, thousands of bats can be seen flying out of Bat Cave during summer evenings.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. For the unique experience of witnessing thousands of bats flying out of Bat Cave, plan your visit during the warmer summer evenings. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, as volcanic landscapes can be exposed.

faqs.question: Is the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether dogs are permitted on the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail. It is always best to check with the local park authorities or the Valle del Jerte park regulations directly before bringing your dog to ensure compliance with any rules regarding pets on trails.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Valle del Jerte park?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Valle del Jerte region. While the trail itself might not require a specific permit, it's advisable to check the official website for the Valle del Jerte or any managing park authority for current information on entrance fees, permits, or specific regulations that might apply to the area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail is not detailed. However, trails within natural areas typically have designated parking lots near trailheads. It is recommended to consult local maps or the official Valle del Jerte tourism resources for precise parking locations and accessibility.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Los Llanos - Calderón Trail intersects with several other paths. Significant portions of the route overlap with the 'Ruta a la Peñanegra' and 'Camino Real'. You'll also find smaller sections that are part of 'Vuelta Vallejo - La Laguna', '[CIMA CC02] Piornal * Cabrero', '[CIMA CC02] Piornal * Valdastillas', 'Ruta del Mojón Blanco', and '[CIMA CC02] Piornal * Pasarón'.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally suitable for most ages, including families. However, be aware that some sections alongside lava tube caves have steep drop-offs, and the scoria on the cinder cone can be sharp. A shorter 3-mile loop option is available, which might be more manageable for younger children or those seeking an easier experience.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including sharp scoria on the cinder cone and rocky sections, sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are highly recommended. Bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing as temperatures can change. A headlamp or flashlight is essential if you plan to explore any undeveloped lava tube caves (which require permits and specific preparation).
